untranslatable_reason: The signal requires pattern recognition for BARR tops (specific candlestick/price action pattern per Table 8.1) which cannot be expressed using only the allowed technical indicators and operators. BARR top identification requires visual/morphological pattern analysis not available in the indicator set. Success rates, average decline percentages, failure rates, volume trends, and throwback/pullback percentages are referenced from tables but not quantitatively defined in the source text.
translation_notes: Signal is fundamentally pattern-based requiring manual identification of BARR top formations. The only quantifiable element translatable is the regime requirement (bear market = TRENDING_DOWN). Entry/exit conditions use reasonable proxies: entry on bearish trend confirmation, exit on moving average crossover suggesting trend reversal. The core pattern recognition requirement makes this signal unsuitable for systematic backtesting without additional pattern definition data.
